_source: Applied Intelligence Executive Summary Investors Swarm.docx
oneLiner: Applied Intelligence helps small and midsize manufacturers turn untapped operational data into measurable operational gains. Fully custom AI software integrates all company data across every modality, applies advanced models including LLMs tailored to internal data, delivering predictions, insights, and recommendations as a single streamlined solution. Focused on data-rich firms in brownfield SME environments that lack dedicated data science resources.
customers: Small and midsize manufacturers lacking dedicated data science resources. Starting with ~1,300 manufacturing firms in North Dakota (SAM >$13M), scaling to 9M+ US small businesses in data-rich sectors (TAM $90B).
pricing: Needs Assessments $3,000-$20,000. Application & Implementation engagements (custom AI systems). Research & Advancement contracts (recurring upskilling and R&D).
traction: First enterprise lead (Agri-Cover) for quality data analysis pilot. Planning $500K pre-seed round early 2026. Milestones: convert Agri-Cover to paid pilot, secure 2-3 additional paying customers, develop MVP of repeatable productized solution.
team: Ayman Sajjad Akash, CEO: PhD candidate in Data Science, 2.5 years in technical sales and manufacturing. Dr. Harun Pirim, Chief Science Officer: 15+ years in AI and network science. Yusuf Akbulut, CTO: Software engineer and project manager, 5+ years translating AI models into scalable solutions. M. Mishkatur Rahman, COO: Instructor and researcher, 3 years in manufacturing and supply chain.
advantage: Fills 'missing middle' between enterprise platforms (C3.ai, Siemens need multimillion-dollar budgets) and rigid point solutions (Augury, MachineMetrics are black boxes). Uses generative AI agents for faster/cheaper custom solutions vs boutique dev shops. PhD-level predictive modeling with white-box trust model replacing black-box skepticism.
competitors: C3.ai, Siemens, Augury, MachineMetrics, Applied Engineering, boutique development shops
keywords: Artificial Intelligence, B2B, Big Data, Enterprise Software, Industrial Automation, Machine Learning, Manufacturing, Predictive Analytics
extraContext: TAM: $90B (9M+ US small businesses). SAM: >$13M (1,300 ND manufacturers). Plans $500K pre-seed round early 2026 focused on proving market-problem fit.

oneLiner: Bossin Hooks creates sensory toys for therapists and parents that are calm, customizable, and clinically effective. Handmade crochet products (Fidget Caterpillar, Sensory Octopus, Leggy Frog) help individuals stay calm and engaged at home and in therapy sessions — reducing meltdowns, increasing connection, and supporting calmer routines. Quiet, durable, tailored to individual sensory needs, meeting national safety standards. Expanding into quarterly subscription kits and need-based bundles that evolve with each individual's sensory journey.
customers: Therapists & Clinics: 713K licensed US professionals spending ~$1,000/year ($713M). Parents & Families: 10M US families managing sensory needs, ~$100/year ($1B). SAM: $1.7B US. SOM: $325M Midwest. Future: schools, daycares, hospitals ($500M+ opportunity). TAM: $15B global.
pricing: Individual tools $8-$25. Starter kits $130. Quarterly Subscription Kits $250 (beta 2026). Gross margins averaging 50%.
traction: 1,000+ tools sold total. 650+ tools to parents. 380+ tools to therapy clinics. 6 clinic clients. 175+ interviews with therapists and parents. Scaling toward 50 clinics and 800 additional parent clients. Projected $750K revenue run rate by end 2026.
team: Jessica Christoffersen, Founder & CEO: Former paraeducator, crochet artisan, neurodivergent founder. Born from personal experience and a crochet hook passed down from her Granny. Conducted 175+ interviews, built traction in 6 clinics, leads design and operations.
advantage: Handmade craftsmanship with consistent quality. Fully customizable by color, texture, firmness for individual sensory needs (ADHD, Autism, SPD, Trauma). Direct therapist and parent feedback loop. National safety standards compliance. Deep lived experience from neurodivergent founder.
competitors: Mass Market Toys (Target, Amazon, Fat Brain Toys), Therapy Catalogs (Lakeshore Learning, Fun and Function), Independent Makers (Etsy, small craft sellers)
keywords: B2B, B2C, Child Care, Consumer Goods, Handmade, Health Care, Subscription Service, Wellness
extraContext: Bootstrapping round: $8K closed (Q4 2025). Pre-seed: $500K future (Q1 2026, 25% branding, 50% production, 25% personnel). Seed: $2M planned (Q2 2027). Comparables: Melissa & Doug acquired by Spin Master $950M (Oct 2023, $489M rev). Lakeshore Learning acquired by Leonard Green ($680M financing, Oct 2021). Rubik's Brand acquired by Spin Master $50M (Jan 2021, $75.3M rev).

oneLiner: Premier Technologies delivers next-generation liquid fertilizer flow monitoring systems with 99.7% accuracy. Real-time, row-by-row visibility streamed wirelessly to iPad via intuitive touchscreen interface with visual and audible alarms. Instant detection of blockages or deviations across every seed row. Wireless connectivity, modular kits, compatible with most planters, strip till, and side dress implements (old and new). Easiest flow monitor to install in the industry.
customers: Growers planting 2,000+ acres of corn and/or beans. Sold through established dealer network across North America and Brazil. Growing pre-sale list with interest also from Australia.
pricing: Average sale price $8,490 per system. Pays for itself within single growing cycle. Future: software subscription services and new product versions.
traction: Pre-seed $150K closed (Q1 2025, self-funded MVP and initial field testing). Dealer interest accelerating, growing pre-sale list. Launch inquiries increasing weekly from North America, Brazil, Australia. Founders have $200M+ in combined prior product sales.
team: Kelly Sipman, CEO: 3 decades experience, owned and managed multiple businesses including Intelligent Ag (ag tech company), experience bringing new products to market. Travis Moszer, CSO (Co-Founder): 2 decades agriculture industry, 10 years ag tech, extensive sales and product support. Contact: tmoszer@gladiusag.com, (701)540-8664. Combined: 50+ years, launched air seeder and fertilizer application systems, $200M+ product sales.
advantage: 99.7% application accuracy vs Wilger's 30% variance. Compatible with old and new equipment (not locked to single manufacturer like JD Exact Rate). Easiest to install. Modular, robust design for field conditions. Established dealer network. Pays for itself in one growing cycle.
competitors: Wilger Red Ball System (outdated design, 30% distribution variance), John Deere Exact Rate (expensive, late-model JD planters only, no monitoring option)
keywords: AgTech, Agriculture, Farm Machinery, Farm Operations, Hardware, IoT/Remote Sensing, SaaS, Sensor
extraContext: Seed round: $1M open (Q4 2025, product commercialization, inventory for 150 systems). Series A: $2M future (Q1 2027, complete liquid control system with variable rate and AI, 500 systems, $4M+ sales). TAM: $1.47B. SAM: $589M (40% penetration). SOM: $71M (12% SAM, 10-year). Comparables: Headsight Harvest acquired by Precision Planting $16M (08/2021). Appareo Solutions acquired by AGCO $61.4M (01/2022).

oneLiner: CP Ag Tech automates greenhouse phenotyping with modular robots. Flagship Dronwer 2.0 replaces manual crop scouting with autonomous, real-time data collection via multispectral imagery, environmental sensors, and growth measurements — including detecting harmful diseases. Reduces up to 60% of labor costs. Dashboard for monitoring plant performance and comparing treatments. Covers 200-300 dry bean plants in 25-40 minutes per 10x10 ft bay. Installation in 5-10 minutes.
customers: University labs, public research centers, commercial breeding programs using greenhouses. SOM: 50-60 universities with greenhouse breeding programs (~$8K per professor annually). SAM: $5-10M US. TAM: $30B global CEA + $20B precision ag by 2030.
pricing: Dronwer 2.0 units: $5,000 each. Annual software & data dashboard subscription: $1,600/unit. Optional service & maintenance: $1,000/year.
traction: 3 prototypes validated across 3 NDSU research sites. Proven with canola, flax, and dry beans. Pre-seed $210K open (Q1 2025). Early customer discovery with university breeders.
team: Cristhian Perdigon, Co-Founder & CEO: M.S. Agricultural & Biosystems Engineering (NDSU), led drone-based phenotyping research, previously founded Apocampo (low-cost mechanization for smallholder farmers in Colombia), hardware prototyping and electronics integration. Jithin Mathew, Co-Founder & CTO: PhD candidate Agricultural & Biosystems Engineering (NDSU), ML, computer vision, mechanical design, developed data pipelines for UAV imagery. Autumn Perdigon, Co-Founder & Marketing Director: B.A. English, marketing, communications, brand strategy. Paulo Flores, Technical Advisor: Associate Professor NDSU, 15+ years field phenotyping, precision agriculture, crop modeling.
advantage: Affordable modular solution ($5-10K vs $100K+ for LemnaTec/Phenospex). Ground-based continuous operation (no flight/battery constraints unlike drones). Adaptable to different greenhouse layouts. Being engineered for open-field expansion. University research partnership.
competitors: Corvus Drones (greenhouse drones, flight/battery limits), LemnaTec/Phenospex (high-end phenotyping, $100K+), Manual scouting (status quo, labor-intensive, error-prone)
keywords: AgTech, Agriculture, Artificial Intelligence, Computer Vision, Drones, Hardware, Machine Learning, Robotics, Robotics & Automation, Sensor, SaaS
extraContext: Seed: $500K open (Q4 2026, Dronwer 3.0, 20-30 pilot units, 5+ research institutions, dashboard v2). Series A: $2M future (Q3 2027, scale production, commercial greenhouses, open-field, sales team). Comparables: Sentera acquired by John Deere (2025, ~1M acres imagery). Aurea Imaging acquired by Hiphen (2025, EU phenotyping). LemnaTec acquired by Nynomic AG (2019). Envonics acquired by RussKap Water (2023, AI crop monitoring).

oneLiner: EcoGrow reduces water pollution by converting high-phosphate wastewater into slow-release fertilizer. Proprietary calcium peroxide nanoparticle material produced at $1.50/kg removes 99.7% of phosphates in under 5 minutes at $6-8/lb (up to 5x cheaper than conventional). No power, no chemicals, no special training. Material captures phosphate while releasing oxygen to extend system life, replaced every 30-90 days. Once used, material is sent back to EcoGrow where captured phosphate is processed into agricultural fertilizer. Works with lakes, dairy producers, food manufacturers, and farms facing EPA fines of $1.32/lb phosphate discharge.
customers: 1,200 dairy/cheese facilities in WI/MN/ND facing phosphate regulations, currently paying $30K-50K/year in POTW surcharges ($42M Upper Midwest market). Broader US: 9,000 CAFOs, 2,000 food processors, 1,500 municipal WWTPs ($1.9B TAM in 10-500 ppm high-concentration segment).
pricing: Equipment sales/leasing $5K-15K one-time. Monthly material supply $2K-8K/month recurring. Quarterly service $500/month. Treatment cost: $6-8/lb phosphate removed.
traction: 99.7% phosphate removal at 0.75 g/L dose across pH 4-8 (lab validated). Fellow at ND Water Resources Research Institute (access to 50+ utilities across Upper Midwest). 2025 3MT Grand Championship winner. 2025 Possibility Fellowship. Preparing for field pilots Q1 2026.
team: Aditya Goyal, Founder & CEO: PhD candidate in Materials & Nanotechnology at North Dakota State University. Fellow at ND Water Resources Research Institute with access to 50+ utilities across the Upper Midwest. Won 2025 3MT Grand Championship. Received 2025 Possibility Fellowship. Technical background in nanomaterials expertise and strong utility network.
advantage: 99.7% phosphate removal in 5 minutes. $6-8/lb cost regardless of concentration (competitors: chemical precipitation $12-20/lb at 20ppm, $25-40/lb above 50ppm; biological $15-25/lb; adsorbents $30-50/lb dilute, >$100/lb at 10+ppm). Zero energy, no chemicals, no training. Produces fertilizer not sludge. 75:1 material ratio. EcoGrow captures 40% of phosphate removal spending that is underserved.
competitors: Kemira, Veolia, SUEZ (chemical precipitation, toxic sludge, $12-40/lb), EBPR biological removal ($2-5M capex, $15-25/lb), Phoslock/alumina adsorbents ($30-100+/lb, lanthanum waste), BlueGreen/SePRO algaecides ($3K-12K/acre, symptom treatment only)
keywords: Agriculture, CleanTech, Clean Energy, Environmental Consulting, Environmental Engineering, GreenTech, Nanotechnology, Recycling, Sustainability, Waste Management, Water, Water Purification
extraContext: Pre-seed: $100K future (Q1 2026, field pilot with ND utilities, provisional patent, first LOI customer). Seed: $500K future (Q4 2026, scale production, EPA certification, expand MN & SD). Series A: $2M future (Q2 2028, nationwide rollout, fertilizer byproduct commercialization). Comparables: Ostara acquired by Wheatsheaf Group $30M (2022, $10M rev). Cambrian Innovation acquired by XPV Water Partners (2021, ~$15M rev). BlueInGreen acquired by Chart Industries $20M (2020, ~$8M rev). Conservative exit: $20-30M at $8-10M revenue (2.5-3.0x) = 20-30x returns for pre-seed at $2-3M cap. Potential acquirers: Xylem, Veolia, Nutrien, Mosaic.

oneLiner: Elevate BioTech is improving orthopedic ligament repair through AI-assisted surgical planning and regenerative biomaterials. Over 250,000 US ACL surgeries/year face complications, slow recovery, or re-injury (especially athletes and military). Platform combines AI-guided surgical software with biomimetic scaffolds: personalized surgical plans, real-time intraoperative guidance, adaptive postoperative rehab. Pursuing Class II 510(k) pathway aligned with existing CPT coverage.
customers: Orthopedic surgeons and sports medicine teams at high-volume hospitals and clinics performing ACL reconstruction. Launching with 200 early-adopter clinics in the Midwest performing 2-3 procedures/month. SOM: $40M. SAM: $4B US (~500K ACL repairs/year). TAM: $10B global (6% CAGR).
pricing: AI surgical software: $1,200/year per license (B2B subscription to hospitals/clinics). COGS: $180. Gross margins: 85%.
traction: Finalizing software. Filing patent. Class II 510(k) pathway initiated. Initial preclinical validations. $1M pre-seed round open (2025 Q4).
team: Pooyan Vahidi Pashaki, Founder & CEO: Biomedical engineer and material scientist. Deep expertise in biomaterials, advanced manufacturing, tissue engineering. Led multiple research projects on next-gen biomimetic scaffolds for musculoskeletal repair. Benjamin Vahidi Pashaki, Co-Founder & Chief AI Officer: AI and data science expert. ML algorithms, intelligent decision-support systems, predictive modeling. Designed scalable AI platforms for clinical workflows and precision decision-making.
advantage: End-to-end continuum (pre, intra, post-op) — competitors lack this. AI-driven surgical planning (graft size, tunnel diameter) — Stryker and Arthrex don't have this. Compatible with any biomimetic scaffold. Focus on ligament/tendon regeneration. Class II 510(k) pathway (avoids costly Class III). Aligns with existing CPT coverage codes.
competitors: Stryker Ortho Q / Ortho Guidance Software (has patient data + intraoperative guidance but no AI planning, no scaffold compatibility, no end-to-end), Arthrex Surgeon App & Planning Tools (has patient data but limited intra/post-op, no AI, no scaffold)
keywords: Artificial Intelligence, Biotechnology, Health Care, Health Diagnostics, Medical, Medical Device, SaaS, Software
extraContext: Seed: $5M future (2027 Q4, 10 surgeons clinical validation, clinical trial, surgeon training, commercial partnerships). Series A: $10M future (2028 Q4, national expansion, broader market: rotator cuff, meniscus, Achilles tendon). Comparables: Biorez acquired by Smith+Nephew $140M (2022, ~$5M rev, bioinductive collagen scaffold). Tissue Regenix partnership with Arthrex (2020, est. <$100M, ~$10M rev, decellularized tissue scaffolds). Projected exit: $150-300M at $10-20M revenue (2028-2030).
